title: Alkermes — Financials
html:
Alkermes Financial Architecture & Budget Intelligence

Governing Thought

The Alkermes account generates ~$456K+ in active SOW value (Lybalvi PET $339K + Vivitrol PET $117K + Aristada SOV ongoing), making it a mid-size, moderate-growth engagement. The financial architecture is straightforward: the DAI organization controls research budgets, SOWs are project-based without enterprise volume commitments, and revenue is concentrated across three PET/SOV engagements. The key financial risk is Vivitrol PET non-renewal (generic entry Jan 2027 reduces Alkermes' incentive to invest in Vivitrol promotional tracking). The key opportunity is LUMRYZ research needs — the Avadel acquisition added a $315-335M narcolepsy brand with zero current ZoomRx research support. Understanding Alkermes' market research budget allocation, particularly how the LUMRYZ integration affects discretionary research spending, is critical for account planning.

Where to Find Financial Data

System What It Holds Access Update Cadence
Kimble Revenue, project-level financials, effort tracking Internal ZoomRx (web app) Real-time as PMs update
Cluster Management (OneDrive) Alkermes share of cluster revenue OneDrive, Base 1 file Weekly refresh
SOW Files Signed project values, signatories, scope Project folders Per-project

Account Financial Summary

Metric FY25 FY26E Notes
Active SOW Value ~$456K+ [Populate post-renewal] Lybalvi PET + Vivitrol PET + Aristada SOV
Proposed (not signed) $89,500 — Sales Force Tactical Optimization
Profitability [Populate from Kimble] — —
AO [Populate] — —
AD Sara Farina (SM) — —

Revenue by Engagement

Engagement Brand SOW Value Service Type Waves/Duration Status
LYBALVI PET Lybalvi $338,701 Multichannel Promotional Effectiveness Tracking 2 full + 2 partial waves Active
VIVITROL PET Vivitrol $117,435 Promotional Effectiveness Tracking 1 wave, 100 interactions Active
ARISTADA SOV Tracker Aristada [Populate — ongoing contract value] Share of Voice Tracker Quarterly, 125 HCPs/wave Active (ongoing since pre-2021)
Sales Force Tactical Optimization Lybalvi $89,500 Tactical optimization [Populate] Proposed — not signed

Revenue Concentration Analysis

Dimension Current
Lybalvi share of SOW value ~74% ($339K of ~$456K)
Top engagement dependency Lybalvi PET = 74% of total
Number of active brands 3 (Lybalvi, Vivitrol, Aristada)
Brands with no ZoomRx work LUMRYZ (acquired Feb 2026)

Key risk: Revenue is heavily concentrated on Lybalvi PET. If that engagement doesn't renew or scope reduces, the account contracts significantly. The Vivitrol PET is also at risk given generic entry in Jan 2027.

Revenue Bridge: Maintaining & Growing the Account

Baseline (Maintain)

Engagement FY26 Estimate Confidence Rationale
Lybalvi PET renewal ~$340K HIGH Cobenfy competition intensifying; Alkermes needs ongoing SoV tracking
Aristada SOV renewal ~$50-75K (est.) HIGH Long-running tracker, institutional; low effort to maintain
Vivitrol PET renewal ~$117K MEDIUM Generic entry Jan 2027 reduces incentive; but FY2026 is the last full branded year
Baseline total ~$507-532K

Growth Opportunities

Opportunity Est. Revenue Confidence Entry Strategy
Sales Force Tactical Optimization (Lybalvi) $89,500 MEDIUM Already proposed; awaiting client decision
LUMRYZ PET or HCP study $100-200K LOW-MEDIUM New acquisition; no relationship with sleep medicine team yet; need to map contacts
Cobenfy-specific deep-dive research $50-100K LOW Depends on whether Alkermes wants more than PET-level Cobenfy tracking
Lybalvi bipolar I positioning study $75-150K LOW Dependent on Cobenfy BPD trial readout creating urgency

The Gap

  • Vivitrol PET non-renewal risk (~$117K) must be offset by new work, ideally LUMRYZ entry
  • SFTO conversion ($89.5K) is the most immediate growth opportunity
  • LUMRYZ research is the highest-potential new vector but requires contact development

Pricing Architecture

Observed Pricing by Engagement Type

Service Observed Range Notes
PET (Multichannel) $338K (4 waves) ~$85K/wave; premium for multichannel scope
PET (Standard) $117K (1 wave, 100 interactions) Competitive pricing for single-wave PET
SOV Tracker [Populate] Quarterly, 125 HCPs/wave; likely $50-75K/year
SFTO (proposed) $89.5K One-time tactical optimization

Pricing Heuristics

  • Alkermes appears to buy research at standard ZoomRx rates without enterprise discount pressure
  • SOW-by-SOW model — no volume commitments or annual spend guarantees
  • IQVIA TPA in place, which reduces sample acquisition costs for ZoomRx
  • [Populate: any pricing sensitivity signals from Sara Farina]

Alkermes Market Research Budget Intelligence

What We Know

Data Point Source Confidence
DAI controls MR budgets centrally SOW signatories (Fritch, Stauble) HIGH
Alkermes total MR spend Unknown —
ZoomRx share of wallet Unknown —
Competitor vendors Unknown —
LUMRYZ research budget (if any) Unknown — Avadel integration in progress —

Critical Gaps

  • What is Alkermes' total annual market research budget?
  • What share does ZoomRx hold?
  • Who else does Alkermes use for market research (Kantar, IQVIA Consulting, etc.)?
  • Does the LUMRYZ commercial team have its own research budget or does it roll into DAI?
  • Has Alkermes ever purchased HCPP, ATU, or other non-PET services from anyone?

Alkermes Public Financial Context

Metric FY2025 FY2026E
Total Revenue ~$1.48B $1.73-1.84B
Proprietary Product Net Sales ~$1.2B $1.52-1.60B
GAAP Net Income $241.7M —
Adjusted EBITDA $394.0M —
Cash & Investments $1.3B —
Avadel Acquisition Cost — $2.1B (closed Feb 2026)

Implication: Alkermes is profitable and well-capitalized. The Avadel acquisition was the largest use of cash in company history. Market research budgets are unlikely to be under cost pressure, but management attention is split across integration, CEO transition, and Vivitrol generic preparation.

Financial Watch Items

Vivitrol PET Non-Renewal (HIGH)

  • Generic entry Jan 2027 reduces Alkermes' incentive to invest in branded Vivitrol promotional tracking
  • FY2026 may be the last year of the Vivitrol PET
  • Mitigation: Position the Vivitrol PET as essential for the final branded year; propose a generic-transition competitive study as a successor engagement

Lybalvi PET Renewal (MEDIUM)

  • Lybalvi PET represents 74% of account revenue — must renew
  • Cobenfy competitive dynamics create strong rationale for continued tracking
  • Mitigation: Ensure Q1 2025 wave (first with Cobenfy tracking) delivers high-impact insights that demonstrate clear ROI

LUMRYZ Revenue Opportunity (MEDIUM)

  • New acquisition with no ZoomRx relationship
  • Sleep medicine market may have different research needs than psychiatry
  • Mitigation: Map LUMRYZ commercial team contacts; understand narcolepsy competitive landscape (Xyrem/Xywav from Jazz); propose introductory research

CEO Transition (LOW but watch)

  • Blair Jackson takes over Aug 2026; may reassess vendor relationships
  • Richard Pops' long tenure created institutional stability
  • Mitigation: Ensure Sara Farina has early engagement with post-transition DAI leadership

Financial Questions to Answer

  • What is the Aristada SOV Tracker annual contract value?
  • What is the account's overall profitability in Kimble?
  • Who is the AO (Principal) for Alkermes?
  • What is the FY26 revenue target from the AO plan?
  • Has the SFTO proposal ($89.5K) been signed or declined?
  • What is Alkermes' total MR budget and ZoomRx share of wallet?
  • Does LUMRYZ have a separate research budget?

Accounts Hub | Home
